channel.title: The Genome Brief
channel.description: An in-depth daily brief on viral clinical & wastewater genomics, biotech/pharma business news, AI in biomedicine, and a fun animal story to close. Each topic is explained, not just announced.
channel.itunes:author: Nigel

item.title: Episode 23: The noise that saves malaria parasites
item.description: A Spielmann-lab bioRxiv preprint uses paired isogenic Plasmodium falciparum lines (wild-type K13 vs C580Y) and single-cell Kelch13 protein quantification to show that survival of artemisinin in genetically resistant parasites is governed by stochastic cell-to-cell variation in Kelch13 abundance rather than by the mutation alone — low-Kelch13 parasites survive because Kelch13 mediates hemoglobin uptake and hence heme-driven artemisinin activation, high-Kelch13 parasites die, and each resistance mutation reshapes the population distribution rather than dictating a fixed phenotype, with low-Kelch13 states carrying a fitness cost via prolonged cell cycle — a paradigm-shifting explanation for partial artemisinin efficacy in fully mutated populations and a suggestion that pushing Kelch13 up could re-sensitize resistant parasites without new chemistry. At AAIC 2026 in London, Biogen and Ionis presented Phase 2 CELIA data on diranersen (BIIB080), a tau-mRNA-targeting antisense oligonucleotide, in 416 patients with early Alzheimer's disease across three intrathecal dose regimens over 18 months: the trial technically missed its primary endpoint (dose-response on CDR-SB) — the best clinical effect was seen at the lowest dose (60 mg Q6M), which slowed CDR-SB by 26%, ADAS-Cog13 by 42%, MMSE by 50%, and modified iADRS by 30% vs placebo, comparable magnitude to the anti-amyloid antibodies, with 50–65% reduction in CSF total tau and PET-tau decreases across every brain region and every dose; Biogen is advancing diranersen to Phase 3 — arguably the first credible anti-tau positive cognitive signal in an Alzheimer's randomized trial. AstraZeneca inked a licensing deal with Dizal Pharmaceuticals for ex-China global rights to Zegfrovy (sunvozertinib), an oral irreversible EGFR TKI approved in China and the US for EGFR exon 20 insertion NSCLC post-chemotherapy — $600M upfront, up to $900M in milestones plus tiered royalties (total ~$1.5B) — plugging the exon-20 gap alongside AstraZeneca's osimertinib franchise and continuing the broader trend of Chinese biotech assets moving to Western majors. And a Nature Chemical Biology paper from UCL, ZSL, and Imperial College (Jervis/Tabor, published 14 July 2026) shows that common midwife toads at three recovered Pyrenean lakes survive the amphibian-killing chytrid fungus (Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis) because they deploy a rich set of antimicrobial peptides during the tadpole stage — before the fungus-vulnerable keratinized adult skin emerges — with the team cataloguing 1,152 distinct peptides where only 7 were previously known, a hidden library that could seed a new generation of human antifungals.
item.itunes:summary: Kelch13 stochastics determine artemisinin survival in resistant P. falciparum (Spielmann lab, Bernhard Nocht Institute, bioRxiv 2026-07-13): paired isogenic wild-type vs C580Y parasite lines with single-cell Kelch13 protein quantification show that low-Kelch13 parasites survive drug exposure while high-Kelch13 parasites die, mechanism via Kelch13-mediated hemoglobin endocytosis feeding heme-dependent artemisinin activation; resistance mutations shift the distribution of Kelch13 levels rather than setting a fixed phenotype; low-Kelch13 also carries a fitness cost via increased cell cycle length; implication is that partial artemisinin efficacy persists in fully mutated populations and pushing Kelch13 up could re-sensitize resistant parasites. Biogen/Ionis diranersen (BIIB080) Phase 2 CELIA at AAIC 2026: tau-mRNA-targeting ASO, intrathecal, n=416 early AD (60% MCI/40% mild dementia, ApoE4 69%, no prior anti-amyloid), 18 mo, three doses vs placebo; trial missed primary endpoint (dose-response on CDR-SB) — best clinical effect at lowest dose (60 mg Q6M): CDR-SB slowing 26%, ADAS-Cog13 42%, MMSE 50%, mod iADRS 30%; CSF total tau −50–65% all doses; PET-tau declines every region every dose; Biogen advancing to Phase 3 — arguably first credible anti-tau positive cognitive signal in an AD RCT, natural counterpoint to Eisai etalanetug biomarker data covered in Ep 22. AstraZeneca–Dizal license deal announced 14 July 2026: ex-China global rights to Zegfrovy (sunvozertinib), oral irreversible EGFR TKI for EGFR exon 20 insertion NSCLC (post-chemo approved in China + US, Breakthrough Therapy Designation for 1L in both agencies); $600M upfront + up to $900M milestones + tiered royalties (~$1.5B total); plugs the exon 20 gap alongside AZ's osimertinib franchise; part of the broader Chinese-biotech-to-Western-majors licensing wave. Midwife toad chytrid survival (Jervis/Tabor, UCL/ZSL/Imperial, Nature Chemical Biology, 14 July 2026): four Pyrenean lakes (1 crashing, 3 recovered, all Bd-positive) — surviving toads deploy antimicrobial peptides at the tadpole stage, before keratinized adult skin (Bd's substrate) emerges; 1,152 distinct peptides catalogued, only 7 previously known — a hidden library with potential as human antifungal leads.

item.title: Episode 22: The test that is making MDR-TB
item.description: A prospective cohort of 42,843 rifampin-susceptible TB patients diagnosed in Ho Chi Minh City between 2020 and 2024, with paired whole-genome sequencing of index and recurrence isolates, finds that 96% of patients who acquired rifampin resistance already carried undetected isoniazid resistance at diagnosis — a ~100-fold elevated risk — and models project that routine HR-TB diagnosis and treatment would cut new RR-TB incidence in Vietnam by 46% over 10 years, indicting the current Xpert MTB/RIF-only diagnostic paradigm as an active driver of MDR-TB. The DRC Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak reached 1,926 confirmed cases and 702 deaths as of the July 12 WHO update, +120 deaths in five days, with Ituri province accounting for 1,745 cases across 26 of 36 health zones; a companion Toronto/CIHR stochastic network modelling paper finds that improving case detection from 30% to 70% and contact tracing from 30% to 80% cuts deaths ~60% on its own, reactive ring vaccination adds only ~14% incremental benefit assuming 45% rVSV-ZEBOV cross-protection against Bundibugyo, and community vaccination at 50% coverage averts 58–86% of deaths depending on rollout delay. At AAIC 2026 in London, Eisai presented Phase 1b/2 Study 103 data on etalanetug (E2814), a monoclonal antibody targeting the microtubule-binding region of tau: plasma MTBR-tau243 fell 78% at 3 months and >90% at 9 months, CSF fell 62% and 89%, and p-tau205 dropped for the first time in dominantly inherited AD — the cleanest target-engagement package of any anti-tau antibody to date, feeding into the Tau NexGen combination trial with lecanemab. A systematic third-party audit of AlphaFold3 from the Beltrao lab at ETH Zurich tests five application areas (ubiquitinated proteins, TCR-epitope, antibody-antigen, protein-RNA, protein-lipid) and finds uneven performance driven by training-set overlap, with confidence scores failing to distinguish real from decoy RNA partners — a cautionary read for drug-discovery programs that lean heavily on AF3 for antibody and RNA tasks. And a Zoological Journal of the Linnean Society paper from a University of São Paulo team shows that harvestmen — the Opiliones arachnids, not spiders — account for more than half of all independently evolved arthropod paternal care, with 62 new records added in a two-day iNaturalist scan doubling ninety years of literature; the evolutionary mechanism appears to be sexual selection in which females prefer males already guarding eggs.
item.itunes:summary: Vietnam HR-TB → RR-TB (Spies et al., Oxford/HCMC, medRxiv 2026-07-13): 42,843-patient prospective cohort with paired WGS; 96% (49/51) of patients acquiring rifampin resistance had undetected isoniazid resistance at index; weighted risk 2.98% vs 0.03% (RR 105); modelling projects 46% reduction in RR-TB incidence over 10 years in Vietnam with universal HR-TB testing + appropriate treatment; even at 5% HR-TB prevalence, ~26% reduction — the WHO-recommended Xpert MTB/RIF diagnostic paradigm is actively generating MDR-TB. DRC Bundibugyo Ebola July 12 WHO DON update: 1,926 confirmed cases, 702 deaths (+120 deaths in 5 days); Ituri 1,745 cases across 26/36 health zones; Uganda 20 cases/2 deaths, no new since 21 June; U.S. entry restrictions expire July 21. Bogoch et al. medRxiv modelling of the same outbreak (calibrated through July 5): case detection 30→70% + contact tracing 30→80% cuts deaths ~60% alone; reactive ring vaccination at 45% assumed cross-protection adds only ~14% incremental; community vaccination at 50% coverage averts 58–86% of deaths depending on rollout delay. Eisai etalanetug (E2814) Phase 1b/2 Study 103 at AAIC 2026 (July 13): anti-MTBR tau antibody targeting seed propagation; plasma MTBR-tau243 −78% at 3 mo, >90% at 9 mo; CSF −62% and −89%; p-tau217/181/231/205 and total tau all reduced (p-tau205 a first for anti-tau in DIAD); DIAN network collaboration, Tau NexGen combination with lecanemab ongoing, Phase 2 in sporadic AD, FDA Fast Track Sept 2025. AlphaFold3 audit (Follonier/Beltrao, ETH Zurich, bioRxiv 2026-07-13): systematic third-party benchmark across five application areas — ubiquitinated proteins, TCR-epitope, antibody-antigen, protein-RNA, protein-lipid; performance uneven and heavily training-set-dependent; AF3 confidence fails to separate real from decoy RNA partners; less generalisable than AF2. Harvestmen (Opiliones, not spiders) account for >half of independently evolved arthropod paternal care; two-day iNaturalist scan by University of São Paulo team added 62 new records, doubling 90 years of literature; evolutionary origin traced to sexual selection where females prefer already-guarding males (sexy-dad hypothesis).

item.title: Episode 21: Why TB hits men harder
item.description: A Bishai/Klein bioRxiv preprint posted July 11 uses the Four Core Genotype mouse model (which decouples XX/XY chromosomes from gonadal sex) plus adoptive CD4+ T-cell transfer to show that female-derived CD4+ T cells confer TB protection to susceptible XY males independently of chromosome complement — via CXCR3-directed homing and CD40L-dependent organization of pulmonary B-cell follicles, with B-cell depletion revealing sex-specific failure modes (T-cell disorganization in females, exaggerated neutrophil/NET-driven inflammation in males). AstraZeneca and Ionis' Phase 3 CARDIO-TTRansform trial of eplontersen (a transthyretin antisense oligonucleotide) failed its primary composite of all-cause mortality and cardiovascular events in ATTR-CM patients over ~140 weeks: 57% of patients were on TTR stabilizers (tafamidis or acoramidis) at baseline and another 24% initiated during the trial, and eplontersen showed a 29% reduction in the primary endpoint in stabilizer-naive patients but no effect in stabilizer-treated patients — Ionis fell 21%, AstraZeneca 8%, and Alnylam's vutrisiran (Amvuttra) is now the sole TTR-knockdown drug in ATTR-CM. Goodfire and Mayo Clinic collaborators (bioRxiv, updated July 11) train a covariance probe on Evo 2 (7B-parameter genomic foundation model) that achieves 0.997 overall AUROC on 833k ClinVar SNVs and 0.991 zero-shot AUROC on 74k indels — a unified framework across missense, silent, splice, UTR, and non-coding variants — and couples it with 357 supervised annotation-disruption probes plus LLM synthesis to produce per-variant natural-language mechanistic hypotheses, available for 4.2M ClinVar variants at the EVEE web application. And Neil the Seal, a one-ton southern elephant seal (Mirounga leonina), has returned to Tasmania to undergo his annual catastrophic molt — a whole-body skin-and-fur shed elephant seals must complete on land in ~30 days — becoming a global folk hero along the way.
item.itunes:summary: Bishai/Klein bioRxiv (2026-07-11): Four Core Genotype mice + adoptive CD4+ T-cell transfer show that female-derived CD4+ T cells confer TB protection independently of sex chromosome complement, via CXCR3 homing, CD40L-driven pulmonary B-cell follicle organization, and Bcl6+ CD4+ T-cell responses; B-cell depletion collapses T-cell architecture in females and unleashes neutrophil/NET-driven inflammation in males — an intrinsic-programming + spatial-organization explanation for the ~2:1 male-biased TB mortality that has puzzled epidemiologists for a century. AstraZeneca/Ionis eplontersen (TTR ASO) Phase 3 CARDIO-TTRansform failure in ATTR-CM (July 11): missed the primary composite of all-cause mortality + CV events over 140 weeks; 57% baseline + 24% incident use of TTR stabilizers (tafamidis/acoramidis) diluted signal — 29% reduction in stabilizer-naive patients, no effect in stabilizer-treated; Ionis -21%, AZ -8%, Alnylam vutrisiran (Amvuttra) becomes the sole TTR-knockdown drug in ATTR-CM. Goodfire + Mayo Clinic bioRxiv (updated 2026-07-11): covariance probe on Evo 2 (7B-parameter genomic foundation model) achieves 0.997 overall AUROC on 833k ClinVar SNVs and 0.991 zero-shot AUROC on 74k indels across all consequence types; paired with 357 annotation-disruption probes and LLM synthesis to produce per-variant mechanistic hypotheses (e.g., a BRCA1 intronic variant flagged as coordinated splice acceptor collapse). All 4.2M ClinVar variants exposed at evee.goodfire.ai. Neil the Seal, ~1-ton southern elephant seal (Mirounga leonina), returns to Tasmania for annual catastrophic molt — a whole-body ~30-day shed that keeps deep-diving pinnipeds on land — a hook for elephant seal physiology (1000m+ dives, 2-hour breath holds, myoglobin O2 stores).

item.title: Episode 20: H5N1 Reaches Australia
item.description: Australia confirmed H five N one high pathogenicity avian influenza (clade 2.3.4.4b) in a greater crested tern — a locally breeding non-migratory seabird — near Robe, South Australia on 10 July 2026, bringing the national count to 13 confirmed detections in wild seabirds across three states (7 Western Australia, 5 South Australia, 1 New South Wales); arrival via transoceanic seabirds (petrels and skuas) circumvented Australia's prior protection from Asian duck flyways, and the key surveillance concern is whether the virus now reaches local duck populations and poultry. The FDA on 10 July 2026 approved enfortumab vedotin (Padcev) plus pembrolizumab (Keytruda) as perioperative treatment for all adult patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer who are candidates for cystectomy, expanding the prior approval from cisplatin-ineligible patients only: KEYNOTE-B15 (n=808) showed 55.8% vs 32.5% pathologic complete response vs gemcitabine-cisplatin (EFS HR 0.53, OS HR 0.65), and KEYNOTE-905 (n=344) showed 57.1% vs 8.6% pCR vs immediate surgery (EFS HR 0.40, OS HR 0.50); the regimen exploits immunogenic cell death in which the Nectin-4-targeting ADC triggers tumor antigen release that is amplified by PD-1 blockade. And a new species of happy-face spider, Theridion himalayana, was described from Uttarakhand, India (above 2,000 m elevation), carrying the same red smiley-face marking as Hawaii's Theridion grallator at 8.5% genetic divergence — convergent evolution whose selective basis remains unknown, with both species sharing an unexplained parallel preference for ginger plants (Hedychium spp.).
item.itunes:summary: Australia H5N1 (clade 2.3.4.4b): 13 confirmed detections in wild seabirds across 3 states as of July 11 2026, first in a locally breeding non-migratory species (greater crested tern, South Australia, July 10); arrival via Southern Ocean seabirds bypassed the lack of Asian-flyway duck overlap that had protected Australia; key risk is spread to local duck populations and poultry. FDA approves Padcev (enfortumab vedotin, Nectin-4 ADC) + Keytruda (pembrolizumab, PD-1 inhibitor) as perioperative treatment for all MIBC candidates for cystectomy, expanding from cisplatin-ineligible patients only — KEYNOTE-B15: 55.8% vs 32.5% pCR vs gemcitabine-cisplatin, EFS HR 0.53; KEYNOTE-905: 57.1% vs 8.6% pCR vs immediate surgery, EFS HR 0.40; mechanism is immunogenic cell death amplified by checkpoint inhibition. Theridion himalayana, new spider species from the Indian Himalayas at 2,000+ m, displays the same red smiley-face marking as Hawaii's Theridion grallator at 8.5% genetic divergence — convergent evolution of an unknown-function pattern, with both species unexpectedly sharing a preference for Hedychium ginger plants.

item.title: Episode 19: Which COVID wave hit kids hardest
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 354 SARS-CoV-2-hospitalized children at Tygerberg Hospital in Cape Town (March 2020 to June 2022) posted to medRxiv finds that pediatric COVID severity varied substantially across variants of concern, with the pre-Omicron Beta and Delta waves — not the ancestral virus or Omicron — carrying the highest burden of severe disease; younger age and admission CRP emerged as independent severity signals, underscoring the need for real-time variant surveillance in low- and middle-income settings where children already carry a heavy respiratory-disease baseline. Rhythm Pharmaceuticals' Phase 3 TRANSCEND trial of setmelanotide (MC4R agonist) in acquired hypothalamic obesity was published in NEJM: 120 patients randomized 2:1 for 52 weeks, 16.5% mean BMI reduction versus 3.3% gain on placebo (19.8% placebo-adjusted), 80% of treated patients hit ≥5% BMI drop, and clinically meaningful hunger improvement — validating the March 2026 FDA approval and demonstrating that hypothalamic-injury-driven obesity responds to restoring the MC4R signaling pathway rather than generalized appetite suppression. A University of Michigan-Kenya Ministry of Health medRxiv paper describes the DMT-PHE AI Agent: a retrieval-augmented generation agent built on Kenya's validated national public-health emergency decision framework, achieving 0.924 weighted concordance with expert gold standards across three simulated outbreak scenarios (unknown etiology, Rift Valley fever, mpox), 78.8% citation coverage with no fabricated sources, and high user trust and time-savings scores from 11 public-health professionals. OpenAI opened its GPT-5.6 family (Sol, Terra, Luna) to general availability on July 9 with a 1M-token context window, February 2026 knowledge cutoff, and API features aimed at agentic workflows; Sol scored 53.6 vs Anthropic Fable 5's 40.5 on OpenAI's Agents' Last Exam, though Fable retains a clear lead on SWE-Bench Pro (80% vs 65%). And a Journal of Fish Biology paper from the University of Hawai'i Manoa and the Minderoo-UWA Deep-Sea Research Centre documents the first-ever footage of a live goblin shark (Mitsukurina owstoni) in its natural habitat — one 2019 ROV sighting at 1,237m near Jarvis Island, and a 2024 baited-camera sighting at 1,997m on the Tonga Trench slope, the deepest confirmed observation of any mackerel shark.
item.itunes:summary: South African pediatric COVID cohort (Tygerberg Hospital, n=354, March 2020–June 2022, medRxiv 2026-07-09): SARS-CoV-2 severity in hospitalized children varied by variant, with the pre-Omicron Beta/Delta waves — not the ancestral virus or Omicron — carrying the highest severe-disease burden; younger age and admission CRP independently flagged severity; abstract-only take (preprint body not yet rendered). Rhythm Pharmaceuticals Phase 3 TRANSCEND trial of setmelanotide in acquired hypothalamic obesity published in NEJM (July 8): 120 patients (2:1 vs placebo, 52 weeks), 16.5% mean BMI reduction vs 3.3% gain on placebo (19.8% placebo-adjusted), 80% hit ≥5% BMI reduction, hunger improved — MC4R agonist restoring hypothalamic satiety signaling lost after craniopharyngioma injury; follows March 2026 FDA approval. University of Michigan + Kenya MoH DMT-PHE AI Agent (medRxiv 2026-07-10): retrieval-augmented generation agent built on Kenya's validated public-health emergency decision framework; 0.924 weighted concordance with expert gold standards across simulated Rift Valley fever, mpox, and unknown-etiology outbreaks; 78.8% citation coverage with no fabricated sources; high trust and time-savings scores; abstract-only. OpenAI GPT-5.6 GA on July 9 — Sol/Terra/Luna, 1M context, February 2026 cutoff, native multi-agent orchestration and programmatic tool calling; Sol 53.6 vs Fable 5's 40.5 on Agents' Last Exam, but Fable dominates SWE-Bench Pro (80% vs 65%). First live goblin shark (Mitsukurina owstoni) footage in natural habitat (Journal of Fish Biology, University of Hawai'i Manoa + Minderoo-UWA): 2019 ROV sighting at 1,237m near Jarvis Island, 2024 baited-camera sighting at 1,997m on the Tonga Trench slope — deepest confirmed mackerel shark observation, expanded species range and ecology for a 125-million-year-old lineage.

item.title: Episode 18: Roche closes the Huntington's book
item.description: A preprint from the University of Alberta, Leiden, and UNC characterizes the two-pronged mechanism of CMX521, a broad-spectrum coronavirus and calicivirus nucleoside analog already through Phase 1 clinical studies: the drug stalls the SARS-CoV-2 RNA-dependent RNA polymerase briefly on incorporation, and template-embedded residues promote reverse copy-back RNA synthesis in the presence of the viral helicase, completely blocking elongation when multiple residues accumulate. Vertex Pharmaceuticals agreed to acquire Crinetics Pharmaceuticals for $10 billion -- a 102% premium and the largest deal in Vertex history -- gaining Palsonify (somatostatin receptor agonist for acromegaly, already on market) and atumelnant (CRF1-targeting agent for congenital adrenal hyperplasia, in late-stage trials), with projected combined peak sales exceeding $5 billion annually. Roche announced it is discontinuing both tominersen (antisense oligonucleotide targeting huntingtin mRNA; failed GENERATION HD2 Phase 2 on clinical functional endpoints despite hitting biomarker goals) and RG6496 (allele-selective ASO; halted in first-in-human after chronic animal dosing safety findings), ending a decade-long Huntington's disease program; UniQure's AMT-130 gene therapy is now the furthest-advanced remaining program. Absentia Labs' AI Digital Liver Model became the first artificial-intelligence drug development tool accepted into the FDA's ISTAND qualification pathway, targeting prediction of drug-induced liver injury before human trials. And a Science study led by Jay Storz at the University of Nebraska characterizes Phyllotis vaccarum, the world's highest-dwelling mammal, which survives Andean volcano summits above 6,700 m at -60 C and 44% sea-level oxygen: high-altitude populations have evolved enhanced skeletal-muscle mitochondrial respiratory capacity for thermogenesis, and both highland and lowland populations carry genes for detoxifying poisonous plant compounds.
item.itunes:summary: CMX521 bioRxiv preprint (Alberta/Leiden/UNC): nucleoside analog already through Phase 1 blocks SARS-CoV-2 RdRp via two independent mechanisms -- brief incorporation-induced pause (~9 s) during synthesis, and complete polymerase stalling plus copy-back RNA synthesis when embedded in the template strand with helicase; broad-spectrum against coronaviruses and caliciviruses. Vertex acquires Crinetics for $10B (102% premium, largest deal in Vertex history): Palsonify (somatostatin receptor agonist, acromegaly, already commercial) + atumelnant (CRF1 antagonist, congenital adrenal hyperplasia, Phase 3); projected $5B+ annual peak sales; strategic diversification beyond cystic fibrosis. Roche discontinues tominersen (GENERATION HD2 Phase 2 failed on functional/cognitive endpoints despite huntingtin biomarker reduction) and RG6496 (Phase 1 halted after 3 patients due to chronic animal dosing safety findings); UniQure AMT-130 gene therapy now furthest advanced in Huntington's pipeline. Absentia Labs Digital Liver Model: first AI drug development tool accepted into FDA ISTAND qualification pathway for predicting drug-induced liver injury (DILI); mechanistic model combining liver biology with drug-response data. Science (Storz group, Nebraska): Phyllotis vaccarum, world's highest-dwelling mammal, survives above 6,700 m at -60 C / 44% O2; evolved enhanced skeletal-muscle mitochondrial respiratory capacity; both high- and low-altitude populations carry plant-toxin detoxification genes -- broadest altitudinal range of any mammal.

item.title: Episode 17: The Ebola net catches Marburg
item.description: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in DRC has intensified to 1,708 confirmed cases and 580 deaths as of July 7, with only 32% of new confirmed cases arising from known contacts — meaning the transmission chain has largely escaped the tracing net. Enhanced surveillance built for the Ebola response in Uganda's Kyegegwa District has now caught something unexpected: a confirmed Marburg virus case in a 17-month-old child, detected because the Ebola dragnet was already looking for filovirus illness. Marburg is a close filovirus cousin of Ebola with ~50% case fatality in outbreak settings, no licensed vaccine, and a reservoir in Egyptian fruit bats. As of the latest update, contacts are being monitored and no secondary cases have been confirmed — but the source exposure remains unknown. Novartis announced it is acquiring Myricx Bio for up to US$1.5B to gain a novel antibody-drug conjugate payload class built on N-myristoyltransferase (NMT) inhibitors — a fundamentally different mechanism from the microtubule-disrupting and topoisomerase-one payloads that dominate current ADCs, with preclinical activity in topoisomerase-resistant tumor models and two lead programs targeting B7-H3 and HER2. Aureka released OpenDDE (Open Drug Discovery Engine), a 655-million-parameter all-atom co-folding model under the Apache 2.0 license, targeting the same structural-prediction niche as AlphaFold 3 but fully open for academic and commercial use, trained on ~414,000 GPU-hours and reporting competitive antibody-antigen co-folding benchmarks. OpenAI's GPT-5.6 family (Sol, Terra, Luna) — previewed to ~20 government-vetted partners for two weeks because of elevated biology and cybersecurity capability scores — opened globally today, July 9. And a University of Hawai'i team reports the first-ever footage of a living goblin shark (Mitsukurina owstoni) in its natural deep-sea habitat, extending the species' known depth range by ~700 meters and providing the first ecological observation of this 125-million-year-old lineage in the wild.
item.itunes:summary: DRC Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak intensifies to 1,708 confirmed cases and 580 deaths; only 32% of new cases arise from known contacts, indicating the transmission chain has largely escaped the tracing net. Uganda's Ebola-focused enhanced surveillance in Kyegegwa District detected an isolated Marburg virus case in a 17-month-old child — a surveillance dividend from the Ebola response infrastructure; source investigation ongoing, no confirmed secondary cases. Novartis acquires Myricx Bio for up to US$1.5B (US$1.1B upfront + US$400M milestones) for a first-in-class ADC payload class built on NMT inhibitors — distinct mechanism from all current approved ADCs, preclinical activity in topoisomerase-resistant tumor models, two lead programs (B7-H3 and HER2). Aureka releases OpenDDE: 655M-parameter all-atom co-folding foundation model, Apache 2.0, ~414K GPU-hours training; competitive antibody-antigen co-folding benchmarks (51–70% top-ranked success rates) and fully open for commercial use — a structural-reasoning alternative to AlphaFold 3. OpenAI GPT-5.6 (Sol/Terra/Luna) opens globally July 9 after two-week government-preview period motivated by elevated SecureBio biology and ExploitBench security scores; Sol priced at $5/$30 per million tokens in/out. First live goblin shark footage in natural habitat: University of Hawai'i team identified two archived and newly collected ROV/lander sightings, extending known depth range ~700m and providing first ecological data on this ~125-million-year-old lineage.

item.title: Episode 16: Bundibugyo Ebola gets its first field test
item.description: The WHO added the first-ever molecular diagnostic test for Bundibugyo virus to its Emergency Use Listing on July 2 — a Shanghai ZJ Bio-Tech RT-qPCR assay whose Bundibugyo-specific primers avoid the cross-reactivity problems of Zaire ebolavirus tests; in the days since, response-network testing capacity has grown roughly ten-fold, from 2–3 labs at 200–400 tests/day to 10 laboratories at over 2,000 tests/day, against a still-growing outbreak now at 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths in the DRC (Ituri Province ~90% of cases) with Uganda having reported no new cases since June 5. AstraZeneca announced today it will pay Sino Biopharmaceutical's subsidiary Chia Tai Tianqing US$200M upfront plus up to US$1.9B in milestones and tiered double-digit royalties (~US$2.1B total) for ex-China global rights to TQC3721, an inhaled dual PDE3/4 inhibitor — same mechanism class as Merck's Ohtuvayre (ensifentrine, first-in-class, FDA-approved June 2024) — currently in Phase 3 as a nebulized suspension and Phase 2 as a dry powder inhaler formulation in China; PDE3 inhibition drives cAMP-mediated bronchodilation of airway smooth muscle, PDE4 inhibition dampens pro-inflammatory cytokines like TNF-α and IL-8, and the dry powder version is the strategic prize as it would offer a portable alternative to nebulized Ohtuvayre. A bioRxiv preprint from Brian Pierce's group at the University of Maryland systematically benchmarks AlphaFold2, AlphaFold3, increased-sampling variants, and related deep-learning methods across antibody-protein, antibody-peptide, and TCR-peptide-MHC complex modeling; AlphaFold3 and expanded sampling improve accuracy over AlphaFold2 defaults but the size of the gain varies by interface class, and pooling predictions across methods lifts near-native success for antibody-peptide complexes from 41% (best single method) to 59% — a practical prescription for antibody and vaccine design pipelines. And a Hebrew University of Jerusalem study in Ecology and Evolution (Sheizaf et al., published July 6) documents woodlice — the Middle Eastern isopod Armadillo sordidus — forming self-sustaining processions of over 5,000 individuals around the circular illuminated boundary cast by streetlights in the Golan Heights, with egg-bearing females as the majority participants; controlled trials rule out magnetic fields and UV, implicating light geometry as the trigger of an army-ant-death-spiral-like emergent behavior driven by artificial light at night.
item.itunes:summary: WHO adds first-ever molecular diagnostic test for Bundibugyo virus (Shanghai ZJ Bio-Tech RT-qPCR) to Emergency Use Listing on July 2; response-network testing capacity has scaled from 2–3 labs at 200–400 tests/day to 10 labs at over 2,000 tests/day against an outbreak now at 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths in DRC (Ituri ~90%), with Uganda no new cases since June 5. AstraZeneca / Sino Biopharm TQC3721 deal (announced July 8): US$200M upfront + US$1.9B milestones + tiered double-digit royalties (~US$2.1B) for ex-China global rights to an inhaled dual PDE3/4 inhibitor for COPD — same mechanism as Merck's Ohtuvayre (ensifentrine); Phase 3 nebulized and Phase 2 dry powder inhaler in China, with the dry powder version the strategic prize versus Ohtuvayre's nebulizer-only formulation. University of Maryland (Pierce group) bioRxiv benchmark of AlphaFold2, AlphaFold3, increased-sampling variants, and related deep-learning methods on antibody-protein, antibody-peptide, and TCR-pMHC complexes: AlphaFold3 + increased sampling improve accuracy but gains vary by interface class, and pooling across methods lifts near-native antibody-peptide success from 41% to 59% — practical prescription for antibody/vaccine design pipelines. Hebrew University of Jerusalem (Sheizaf et al., Ecology and Evolution, July 6): 5,000+ woodlice (Armadillo sordidus), majority egg-bearing females, form self-sustaining circular processions around streetlight illumination boundaries in the Golan Heights; controlled trials rule out magnetic fields and UV — light geometry drives an army-ant-death-spiral-like emergent behavior at the invertebrate scale.

item.title: Episode 15: Omicron's upper-airway trick, decoded
item.description: A preprint from the MRC-University of Glasgow Centre for Virus Research establishes that the H655Y spike mutation shifts SARS-CoV-2 Omicron toward cathepsin-dependent cell entry but is not the determinant of upper-airway tissue tropism — that role belongs to receptor-binding domain mutations in BA.2.86 that override H655Y entirely, mechanistically uncoupling protease preference from tissue tropism. Mpox clade Ib, after establishing local transmission in Madagascar in January 2026, accounted for 780 confirmed cases in Africa during the six-week period April 6 through May 17 — more than any other country including the DRC, whose mpox surveillance is depressed by the concurrent Bundibugyo Ebola response — and has already exported cases to Mauritius and Réunion. The FDA's PDUFA deadline for atacicept, Vera Therapeutics' dual BAFF-and-APRIL-blocking fusion protein for IgA nephropathy, falls today; the ORIGIN 3 Phase 3 trial showed 46% proteinuria reduction and the drug holds Breakthrough Therapy Designation and Priority Review status; if approved it is the first IgA nephropathy therapy to target B cell activation at the cytokine level. A City University of Hong Kong preprint describes VirProtRAG, a retrieval-augmented generation framework that annotates viral protein function by grounding predictions in retrieved scientific literature, adding non-overlapping functional annotations for roughly a third of the 17,484 Swiss-Prot viral proteins beyond existing expert curation. And fossil bee nests — named Osnidum almontei — in the tooth sockets of owl-pellet jawbones from a Hispaniola cave document the first known use of animal bones as bee nesting sites, an unintended ecological relay between owl hunting and solitary bee nest-site selection 20,000 years ago.
item.itunes:summary: Glasgow MRC preprint: SARS-CoV-2 H655Y shifts Omicron to cathepsin-dependent entry but does not determine upper-airway tropism — receptor-binding domain mutations from BA.2.86 are the actual determinant, mechanistically uncoupling protease preference from tissue tropism. Madagascar mpox clade Ib: 780 confirmed cases in six weeks (Apr 6–May 17, WHO SR #66), top mpox country in Africa, with exportations to Mauritius and Réunion and household transmission including children. Atacicept PDUFA today (July 7): dual BAFF/APRIL blocker for IgA nephropathy, 46% proteinuria reduction in ORIGIN 3, first B cell cytokine-targeting therapy for this autoimmune kidney disease. VirProtRAG (City U Hong Kong): retrieval-augmented generation annotates viral protein function from the scientific literature, adding ~33% non-overlapping annotations beyond Swiss-Prot expert curation. Osnidum almontei fossil bee nests from tooth sockets of owl-pellet jawbones in Hispaniola — first known use of animal bones as solitary bee nesting sites, 20,000 years ago.

item.title: Episode 14: A nutritional choke point in malaria transmission
item.description: A bioRxiv preprint from Kim Williamson's group at Weill Cornell with Wageningen and Radboud collaborators identifies PfApiAT2 — one of only about a dozen membrane transporters encoded by Plasmodium falciparum — as a proline-specific transporter that is dispensable in the blood stage but essential for oocyst development in Anopheles mosquitoes; PfApiAT2-knockout parasites form stunted, degenerating oocysts and produce zero sporozoites, and the defect is fully rescued by supplementing infected mosquitoes with free amino acids, pinpointing a transmission-blocking vulnerability at the same protein that mediates resistance to the experimental antimalarial halofuginone. Novartis announced today it will acquire London-based Myricx Bio for up to US$1.5B (US$1.1B upfront plus US$400M in milestones) to add a first-in-class antibody-drug-conjugate payload class built on inhibitors of N-myristoyltransferase (NMT), an enzyme that attaches a 14-carbon myristate lipid to hundreds of human proteins that cancer cells depend on for membrane trafficking and signaling; the payload also triggers the unfolded protein response and reprograms tumor-associated macrophages, giving Novartis two lead ADC programs with a mechanism distinct from the microtubule-disrupting and DNA-damaging payloads currently dominant in the field. A bioRxiv preprint from Maha Farhat's group at Harvard with Tufts and Texas A&M collaborators systematically evaluates three active-learning strategies for whole-cell antibiotic screens, using retrospective Mycobacterium tuberculosis screening data and prospective closed-loop screening against Borrelia burgdorferi (the Lyme disease bacterium); the winning strategy — balancing predicted-hit exploitation with a novelty bonus — takes the experimental hit rate from 0.2% baseline to 1.0% (5x) and, when applied prospectively across diverse chemical space, achieves 11% confirmed activity (a 53-fold enrichment) with 100% Borrelia-selective hits. And a Matsunaga and Nose bioRxiv preprint from the University of Tokyo dissects how the elongated posterior respiratory siphon of rat-tailed maggots (Helophilus virgatus hoverfly larvae) actually deploys — not by hydraulic pressure but by a folding-unfolding mechanism driven by a dense array of ring-shaped muscle fibers that also asymmetrically bend the siphon side-to-side, revealing that the snorkel doubles as a rowing oar for near-surface locomotion.
item.itunes:summary: Weill Cornell/Wageningen/Radboud bioRxiv preprint: PfApiAT2 is a Plasmodium falciparum proline transporter dispensable in blood stage but essential for oocyst development and sporogony in Anopheles mosquitoes — knockouts form stunted, degenerating oocysts and produce zero sporozoites, fully rescued by supplementing mosquitoes with free amino acids; identifies a transmission-blocking vulnerability at the same protein mediating halofuginone resistance. Novartis acquires London-based Myricx Bio for up to US$1.5B (US$1.1B upfront + US$400M milestones) to add a first-in-class ADC payload built on N-myristoyltransferase (NMT) inhibitors — a novel mechanism that disrupts myristate-anchored cancer-signaling proteins, triggers the unfolded protein response, and reprograms tumor-associated macrophages, distinct from existing microtubule/DNA-damaging payloads. Harvard (Farhat) + Tufts + Texas A&M active-learning antibiotic discovery preprint: closed-loop AL against Borrelia burgdorferi lifts hit rate from 0.2% baseline to 1.0% (5x), with 11% confirmed activity (53-fold enrichment) and 100% Borrelia-selective hits when applied prospectively across diverse chemical space; wins by balancing exploitation with a novelty bonus. University of Tokyo bioRxiv preprint (Matsunaga & Nose): rat-tailed maggot (Helophilus virgatus) respiratory siphon deploys via folding-unfolding driven by transversely-oriented ring muscles that also asymmetrically bend the siphon — revealing that the snorkel doubles as a rowing oar for near-surface locomotion, a striking case of functional co-option.

item.title: Episode 13: A second filovirus surfaces in Uganda
item.description: Uganda notified WHO on 30 June 2026 of a confirmed Marburg virus disease case — a 17-month-old child in Kyegegwa District, western Uganda — detected through enhanced surveillance set up for the ongoing Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the DRC; STAT reports two possible cases were under investigation, but no contacts have developed symptoms and Africa CDC characterizes the situation as isolated. The WHO's July 3 update on the Bundibugyo outbreak puts cumulative cases at 1,481 with 454 deaths across the DRC, Uganda, and France; Uganda's last confirmed Bundibugyo case was June 21, and the French imported case (a physician returning from Ituri) has generated 107 contacts in Kinshasa but no secondary spread. An ICDDR,B bioRxiv preprint delivers the first post-pandemic RSV genomic surveillance data from Bangladesh: 49 full RSV genomes (43 RSV-A ON1, 6 RSV-B BA9) from 59 high-viral-load hospital samples collected Aug 2024–Dec 2025 and deposited to GISAID; S276N and S389P substitutions on the F protein sit outside monoclonal-antibody epitopes and don't compromise nirsevimab, clesrovimab, or the F-protein vaccines, but every RSV-A and most RSV-B strains acquired a new N-linked glycosylation site on F — a form of glycan drift worth watching. Roche's Phase 3 Krascendo 1 head-to-head trial of divarasib vs. sotorasib or adagrasib in 338 previously treated KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC patients met both primary (PFS) and key secondary (OS) endpoints with statistically significant improvements over the two approved first-generation KRAS G12C inhibitors; peak sales projected at CHF 1–2.5B and a second-line regulatory filing is expected. A University of Warwick Communications Biology study analyzes 140 laughter sequences from orangutans, gorillas, bonobos, chimpanzees, and humans, finding that all five species produce isochronous — evenly-spaced — laughter, indicating that the rhythmic scaffolding of human laughter has been conserved across roughly 15 million years of great-ape evolution.
item.itunes:summary: Uganda confirmed a Marburg virus case (17-month-old, Kyegegwa District) on June 30, picked up through enhanced Ebola surveillance for the DRC Bundibugyo outbreak; Africa CDC characterizes it as isolated with no contact spread. Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak update (WHO DON, July 3): 1,481 confirmed cases, 454 deaths across DRC, Uganda, and France; Uganda's last case June 21, French imported case (physician returning from Ituri) has 107 Kinshasa contacts and no secondary spread. ICDDR,B bioRxiv preprint: 49 full RSV genomes (RSV-A ON1 94.6%, RSV-B BA9) sequenced by Oxford Nanopore from Bangladeshi hospital samples Aug 2024–Dec 2025 and uploaded to GISAID; S276N (RSV-A, 35%) and S389P (all RSV-B) F-protein substitutions sit outside nirsevimab and clesrovimab epitopes, but all RSV-A gained a new F-protein N-linked glycosylation site — glycan drift to watch. Roche Krascendo 1 Phase 3 (n=338, 2L KRAS G12C NSCLC): divarasib beat sotorasib and adagrasib head-to-head on both PFS and interim OS; regulatory filing expected. Communications Biology (Warwick, De Gregorio et al.): 140 laughter sequences across orangutans, gorillas, bonobos, chimps, and humans are all isochronous — the rhythmic scaffolding of laughter has been conserved for ~15 million years, arguing that vocal-timing control predates speech.

item.title: Episode 12: A new malaria resistance gene sweeps Uganda
item.description: A bioRxiv preprint updated this week from UC San Francisco, UC San Diego, and Ugandan collaborators identifies a new drug-resistance haplotype — PIN — in the Plasmodium falciparum px1 gene that was absent in Uganda in 2004, first appeared in 2008 two years after artemether-lumefantrine became first-line therapy, and has since swept to over 50% prevalence in eastern Uganda and over 80% in northern Uganda by 2024; PIN-carrying parasites show significantly decreased susceptibility to lumefantrine, mefloquine, and dihydroartemisinin — the active form of artemisinin — and the haplotype spreads independently of known K13 artemisinin-resistance mutations. A University of Pennsylvania serological survey of 417 feral and 228 owned cats in the greater Philadelphia area finds only one cat (0.16%) seropositive for clade 2.3.4.4b H5N1 in both binding and neutralization assays, while 20 cats (3.1%) tested positive for SARS-CoV-2, suggesting urban and feral cats are a useful sentinel species and the H5N1 threat in urban cat populations remains low. The FDA has a PDUFA decision due Monday, July 7, for atacicept (Vera Therapeutics) for IgA nephropathy — the most common autoimmune kidney disease — a first-in-class recombinant fusion protein blocking both BAFF and APRIL that reduced proteinuria by 46% in the ORIGIN 3 Phase 3 trial. A bioRxiv benchmark of 54,364 paired Mycobacterium tuberculosis whole-genome sequencing and drug-susceptibility records tests seven AI models across 18 drugs: XGBoost outperforms deep learning by precision-recall metrics, frontline drugs like isoniazid and rifampicin are well-predicted, but newer reserve drugs including bedaquiline, delamanid, and linezolid remain persistently difficult across all models — identifying the data gap and incomplete resistance biology as the key bottleneck. And a Science paper shows bumble bees spontaneously solving a novel object-manipulation task by rolling a ball under a suspended reward to use it as a step-stool — combining two separately learned memories into an unlearned action plan, challenging the assumption that flexible insight-like problem solving requires a large brain.
item.itunes:summary: New Plasmodium falciparum px1 PIN haplotype sweeping Uganda: absent in 2004, first seen 2008, now 50-84% prevalence by 2024; decreases susceptibility to lumefantrine, mefloquine, and dihydroartemisinin independently of K13 artemisinin-resistance mutations. H5N1 and SARS-CoV-2 serosurvey in Philadelphia cats: 0.16% H5N1-positive (1/645), 3.1% SARS-CoV-2-positive (20/645) — urban cats largely spared H5N1, COVID more common. Atacicept PDUFA July 7: first dual BAFF/APRIL inhibitor for IgA nephropathy; 46% proteinuria reduction in ORIGIN 3 Phase 3 trial. TB drug-resistance AI benchmark (54K WGS-pDST records, 7 models, 18 drugs): XGBoost wins precision-recall, deep learning competitive by AUROC; newer drugs (bedaquiline, delamanid, linezolid, clofazimine) remain hard for all models — bottleneck is data and biology, not model architecture. Bumble bees (Science): spontaneous novel object-manipulation task solved by rolling a ball as a step-stool, combining two isolated memories without training — insight-like problem solving in a brain smaller than a sesame seed.

item.title: Episode 11: A first shot at treating Bundibugyo Ebola
item.description: The WHO announced yesterday that patient enrollment has begun in PARTNERS — the Platform Adaptive Randomised Trial for New and Repurposed Filovirus TreatmentS — the first-ever randomized trial of treatments for Bundibugyo Ebola virus, the strain responsible for an ongoing outbreak in Ituri Province, DRC that has now exceeded 1,400 confirmed cases and 440 deaths; the initial arms test Mapp Biopharmaceutical's cross-reactive antibody cocktail MBP134 and Gilead's remdesivir, alone and in combination, against a supportive-care control, coordinated by INRB Kinshasa, ITM Antwerp, and the University of Oxford. Ipsen announced it will acquire Swiss biotech Memo Therapeutics for €200M upfront and up to €700M in total to gain potravitug, the first-in-class monoclonal antibody targeting BK polyomavirus VP1 capsid protein — a virus that reactivates in ~30% of kidney transplant recipients on immunosuppression and destroys grafts through BK polyomavirus-associated nephropathy; the Phase 2 SAFE KIDNEY II trial missed its primary DNAemia endpoint statistically but showed biopsy-proven nephropathy dropping from 51% to 32% in treated patients versus no change on placebo, with pivotal Phase 2/3 planned for later this year. An updated bioRxiv preprint from the Case, Li, and Savinov labs at MIT applies FragFold, an AlphaFold2-based fragment-binder design method, to biomolecular condensates — screening 2,235 possible fragments across four condensate-forming proteins (G3BP1, SARS-CoV-2 nucleocapsid, TDP-43, FAK), testing 18, and getting a 50% experimental hit rate for fragments that either dissolve or enhance condensates; in FAK, the inhibitor identified a previously uncharacterized FERM-kinase domain interaction as essential for phase separation and worked in living mouse cells. And a Frontiers in Conservation Science study from the University of York analyzes nearly 3,500 aggressive wildlife encounters in Canadian national parks from 2010–2023 and finds that elk — not bears — cause 62% of them, with bear incidents concentrated on quiet hikes and elk incidents concentrated on golf courses, ski slopes, and townsites.
item.itunes:summary: WHO announces the first patient enrolled in PARTNERS, a WHO-sponsored platform trial of MBP134 (Mapp Biopharmaceutical's cross-reactive anti-ebolavirus antibody cocktail) and remdesivir, alone and combined, for Bundibugyo virus disease — the first-ever randomized trial for this ebolavirus species — in the ongoing Ituri Province DRC outbreak (1,400+ cases, 440+ deaths). Ipsen acquires Memo Therapeutics for up to €700M (€200M upfront) for potravitug, first-in-class anti-VP1 monoclonal antibody for BK polyomavirus reactivation in kidney transplant recipients; Phase 2 missed primary DNAemia endpoint but biopsy-proven BKPyVAN dropped 51% → 32% in treated group, unchanged on placebo; pivotal Phase 2/3 to start late 2026. FragFold updated preprint (MIT): AlphaFold2-based AI method designs protein fragments that act as monovalent chain-terminators or bridging ligands for biomolecular condensates; 50% hit rate across G3BP1, SARS-CoV-2 nucleocapsid, TDP-43, and FAK from 2,235-fragment computational screen and 18 experimental tests; in FAK the inhibitor identified FERM–kinase domain interaction as essential for phase separation and worked in living mouse cells. University of York analysis of ~3,500 aggressive Canadian national parks wildlife incidents 2010–2023: elk cause 62% (not bears), driven by townsite/adventure-sports overlap and human-as-predator-refuge behavior; bear incidents concentrated in low-noise hiking activities.

item.title: Episode 10: When artemisinin resistance reaches the Horn of Africa
item.description: A UNC-Chapel Hill and Ethiopian collaboration uses CRISPR-Cas9 to introduce the classic Southeast Asian Plasmodium falciparum artemisinin-resistance mutation K13 C580Y into two recently collected Ethiopian clinical isolates and demonstrates via ring-stage survival assay that the mutation is sufficient to confer artemisinin tolerance in contemporary African parasite backgrounds — one of the first causal validations closing off the possibility that C580Y might be silently incompatible with the African genetic background. FDA approves Tregzi, Orca Bio's allogeneic Treg-based cell therapy composed of highly purified regulatory T cells, conventional T cells, and hematopoietic stem cells, for adults with hematologic malignancies undergoing allogeneic HSCT; Phase 3 shows 78% one-year chronic-GVHD-free survival vs 38.4% on standard of care, priced at $428,000. OpenGerminal, a University of Virginia bioRxiv preprint, rebuilds the Germinal antibody-design pipeline with a fully open-source stack (OpenMM, FreeSASA, Biopython, sc-rs) and swaps IgLM for AbLang1, delivering the field's first head-to-head comparison of the two antibody language models — AbLang1 raises the Chai-1 cofolding pass rate from ~19% to ~34% on PD-L1 and from 8% to 25% on IL-3, at ~1.5x per-trajectory compute cost. A University of Bristol paper in Palaeobiology maps the theoretical morphospace of pterosaur wing shapes across nine species and finds that current reconstructions cluster in a tiny corner of the space — a hundred million years of pterosaur evolution across three orders of magnitude of body size is being rendered with essentially one wing profile, because the soft-tissue flight membrane almost never fossilizes and existing reconstructions extrapolate from bones with a single-silhouette assumption.
item.itunes:summary: P. falciparum K13 C580Y functional validation in contemporary Ethiopian clinical isolates: CRISPR-Cas9 editing plus ring-stage survival assays confirm the classic Cambodian artemisinin-resistance mutation confers reduced dihydroartemisinin susceptibility in African parasite backgrounds — the mutation is functional in the Horn of Africa, so whether it spreads is now epidemiology, not molecular. FDA approves Tregzi (Orca Bio): first Treg-cell-based immunotherapy; 78% one-year chronic-GVHD-free survival vs 38.4% standard of care in allogeneic HSCT for hematologic malignancies; $428K price point. OpenGerminal from UVA: open-source rebuild of the Germinal antibody design pipeline, swapping PyRosetta and IgLM for OpenMM/FreeSASA/Biopython and AbLang1 — provides the field's first head-to-head comparison of the two antibody language models, showing AbLang1 raises Chai-1 cofolding pass rates from 19% to 34% on PD-L1 and from 8% to 25% on IL-3. Bristol paper in Palaeobiology: theoretical morphospace analysis reveals pterosaur wing reconstructions across nine species are all clustered at a single silhouette, because the soft-tissue flight membrane rarely fossilizes and reconstructions extrapolate from bones under a single-shape assumption — a hundred million years of flight evolution is being flattened.

item.title: Episode 9: Mpox doubles up in eastern Congo
item.description: A medRxiv preprint from the DRC Institute of Epidemiology and Erasmus Medical Center documents simultaneous Clade Ia and Clade Ib mpox co-circulation in South Kivu province from January through May 2026, complicating diagnosis and outbreak response in the region where Clade Ib first emerged. Zymeworks acquires Theravance Biopharma for $929 million to add YUPELRI (revefenacin), the only nebulizer-delivered long-acting muscarinic antagonist for COPD maintenance, generating approximately $60M annual cash flow to fund its bispecific antibody oncology pipeline. GeneBench-Pro, a new bioRxiv benchmark of 129 expert-reviewed multi-step genomics and quantitative biology problems, finds the best current model (GPT 5.6 Sol Pro) reaches only 31.5% pass rate, with a consistent failure mode of correctly identifying diagnostic signals but failing to propagate their implications to the downstream analysis decision. Anthropic releases Claude Sonnet 5, a midsize agentic model that scores 63.2% on agentic coding benchmarks and slightly outperforms Opus 4.8 on knowledge-work tasks at substantially lower cost, with improved resistance to prompt injection. A Current Biology study shows Octopus bimaculoides can learn to use a mirror to locate prey hidden from direct view, choosing the correct side in 73% of trials — the first demonstration of mirror-guided spatial navigation in any invertebrate.
item.itunes:summary: Mpox Clade Ia + Clade Ib co-circulation confirmed in South Kivu, DRC, Jan–May 2026 — diagnostic and response implications for an already complex outbreak. Zymeworks acquires Theravance for $929M: YUPELRI (revefenacin), only nebulizer-delivered LAMA for COPD, ~$60M annual cash flow funding oncology pipeline. GeneBench-Pro: 129-problem AI benchmark for genomics/bioinformatics reasoning, GPT 5.6 Sol Pro reaches 31.5%, Claude Opus 4.8 reaches 16%, dominant failure is noticing diagnostic signals but not acting on them. Claude Sonnet 5: agentic model matching near-Opus performance at lower cost, improved prompt-injection resistance. Octopus bimaculoides uses mirror-guided navigation to locate hidden prey — first invertebrate shown to use mirrors for spatial reasoning.

item.title: Episode 8: Antibiotic resistance, exported
item.description: A bioRxiv preprint from groups in Chile, Houston, and Argentina shows that NDM-7, an emerging metallo-beta-lactamase variant, is packaged into outer membrane vesicles released by Klebsiella pneumoniae — the vesicles carry catalytically active enzyme into the extracellular space, degrade imipenem and partially restore growth in the last-line antibiotic cefiderocol, and protect susceptible bystander bacteria with no resistance gene of their own; KPC, the other major carbapenemase, stays cell-bound and does not export this way. A Cornell-led medRxiv preprint analyzes 1,235,424 confirmed Brazilian chikungunya cases across 5,570 municipalities from 2015–2025 and shows that transmission and severity are spatially decoupled — the Central-West drives epidemic spread while the Southeast carries the highest case fatality — meaning a simple age-based IXCHIQ vaccination rule would miss 82% of 2024–25 cases. Ipsen announces it will acquire Kartos Therapeutics for up to $1.75 billion (US$450M upfront, up to US$1.3B in milestones) to develop navtemadlin, an oral MDM2 inhibitor that reactivates wild-type p53 as a ruxolitinib add-on in intermediate/high-risk myelofibrosis, with Phase 3 POIESIS topline expected in 2027. A UCSF arXiv preprint builds clinical reasoning graphs to evaluate the consistency of LLM diagnostic reasoning across 750 traces from five frontier models and finds "competence without consistency" — 60–70% diagnostic accuracy paired with reasoning graphs that look essentially identical for clinically similar and clinically dissimilar cases. And a nine-year camera-trap study at Stanford's Jasper Ridge Biological Preserve shows that mountain lions ranging into a sub-five-square-kilometer suburban preserve have rearranged the food web from top to bottom — fewer deer, more woody plants, less coyote and bobcat activity, more foxes, and brush rabbits shifting to mornings.
item.itunes:summary: NDM-7 carbapenemase rides outer membrane vesicles: emerging Klebsiella pneumoniae variant exports active enzyme that degrades imipenem and partially defeats cefiderocol in extracellular space, protects susceptible bystanders without gene transfer; KPC stays cell-bound. Brazil chikungunya allocation framework from Cornell: 1.23M cases across 5,570 municipalities, 2015–2025; transmission epicenter shifted Northeast to Central-West, mortality concentrated in Southeast/South; age-only IXCHIQ rollout would miss 82% of recent cases. Ipsen acquires Kartos for up to $1.75B (US$450M up front) for navtemadlin, oral MDM2 inhibitor reactivating wild-type p53 in myelofibrosis as ruxolitinib add-on; Phase 3 POIESIS readout 2027. UCSF clinical reasoning graphs: five frontier LLMs across 750 diagnostic traces show 60–70% accuracy with reasoning patterns indistinguishable between clinically similar and dissimilar cases — competence without consistency. Jasper Ridge mountain lions: 9 years of camera-trap data in <5 km² Stanford preserve show full trophic cascade — fewer deer, recovered woody plants, displaced coyotes/bobcats, more foxes, brush rabbits shift to mornings.

item.title: Episode 7: Bird flu hits the last continent
item.description: H5N1 avian influenza confirmed on mainland Australia for the first time — a brown skua at Cape Le Grand National Park becomes the first mainland case on the final inhabited continent after a four-year global expansion that has killed hundreds of millions of birds and tens of thousands of marine mammals. A six-year retrospective from the University of Washington Clinical Virology Laboratory finds 47.8% of clinically submitted herpes simplex virus samples harbor acyclovir resistance mutations, including 23 novel mutations without prior characterization, raising questions about how to interpret genotypic testing when the biology of a variant is unknown. The FDA accepts for the third time Replimune's biologics license application for RP1 (vusolimogene oderparepvec) combined with nivolumab for anti-PD-1-failed advanced melanoma — an oncolytic modified herpes virus paired with checkpoint blockade — with an August 2 decision date and advisory committee expected in late July. And a newly described Australian spider, the ballista spider from the genus Propostira, has evolved a spring-powered silk trap that launches green tree ants more than thirty centimeters into the air at 140 G, making it the only spider known to specialize exclusively on one prey species.
item.itunes:summary: H5N1 reaches mainland Australia — brown skua confirmed at Cape Le Grand National Park, virus now on every inhabited continent after four-year global expansion. UW Clinical Virology Lab: 47.8% of HSV acyclovir resistance tests positive over six years, 23 novel uncharacterized mutations identified. Replimune RP1 + nivolumab BLA accepted (third try): oncolytic modified HSV for anti-PD-1-failed melanoma, August 2 PDUFA date, advisory committee in late July. Ballista spider Propostira sp.: spring-loaded silk catapult launches green tree ants at 140 G, only spider known to hunt a single prey species exclusively.

item.title: Episode 6: Surveillance goes airborne
item.description: Researchers at Wisconsin-Madison and Missouri demonstrate that the Cepheid GeneXpert, a point-of-care PCR device deployed in over 11,000 facilities worldwide, can detect SARS-CoV-2 and influenza A from air samples in congregate settings — offering a path toward ambient surveillance without new infrastructure. Moderna discloses mRNA-6007, an in vivo CAR-T program for autoimmune disease that uses lipid nanoparticles to deliver CAR-encoding mRNA directly into patients' T cells, enabling transient B cell depletion without ex vivo cell manufacturing. KG-TRACE, a neuro-symbolic AI framework, combines a neural network with the WHO mutation knowledge graph to predict tuberculosis drug resistance from whole-genome sequencing data and flags uncertainty when mutations fall outside established biology — achieving AUROC 0.9760 for isoniazid on the CRyPTIC cohort. And a juvenile great white shark caught by Spanish fishermen in the Mediterranean revives a 160-year debate about a ghost breeding population persisting in waters where great whites have no right to be.
item.itunes:summary: GeneXpert air surveillance: point-of-care PCR detects SARS-CoV-2 and influenza A from air samples in congregate settings — 281 field samples, fair-to-moderate concordance with RT-qPCR, leveraging 11,000+ existing deployments worldwide. Moderna mRNA-6007 in vivo CAR-T: LNPs deliver CAR-encoding mRNA to T cells in the body, transiently depleting CD19-positive B cells for lupus and other B cell-mediated autoimmune diseases without ex vivo cell manufacturing. KG-TRACE neuro-symbolic AI: WHO mutation knowledge graph combined with neural network for interpretable TB AMR prediction, AUROC 0.9760 for isoniazid on CRyPTIC cohort, epistemic trust gate flags uncertain predictions. Ghost great whites: juvenile shark in Mediterranean confirms 160-year ghost population, critically endangered but apparently still breeding.

item.title: Episode 5: Ebola on a plane
item.description: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak has confirmed its first commercial-flight case in Europe: a humanitarian doctor flew Air France from Kinshasa on June 23 while experiencing only mild headaches and tested positive in France on June 24, with no Bundibugyo-specific vaccine or treatment available and the DRC outbreak now at 1,155 confirmed cases and 304 deaths. The FDA approved olezarsen (Tryngolza) for severe hypertriglyceridemia — the first drug with a labeled indication for reducing acute pancreatitis risk — an antisense oligonucleotide that silences APOC3, the double brake on blood-fat clearance, cutting triglycerides 50-70% and pancreatitis events by 85%. A new arXiv paper shows simulation-based inference with neural posterior estimation can replace MCMC for Bayesian epidemic model calibration, enabling parameter estimates in seconds rather than hours for near-real-time outbreak response. And Heliconius butterflies reveal a 25-fold lifespan difference from closely related species, with older individuals showing no grip-strength decline — evidence that aging itself has slowed, not just been nutritionally extended.
item.itunes:summary: Bundibugyo Ebola: first commercial-flight case in Europe — humanitarian doctor flew Air France from Kinshasa on June 23, tested positive in France June 24; outbreak now 1,155 DRC cases, 304 deaths, no Bundibugyo vaccine or treatment. FDA approves olezarsen (Tryngolza) for severe hypertriglyceridemia — first drug labeled for pancreatitis risk reduction, ASO targeting APOC3, 85% reduction in pancreatitis events. Simulation-based inference vs. MCMC for epidemic model calibration: neural posterior estimation delivers seconds-fast Bayesian inference for real-time outbreak response. Heliconius butterflies: 25-fold lifespan difference from closely related species, heritable slowing of aging independent of pollen diet.

item.title: Episode 4: Ebola at the border
item.description: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda — declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern in May 2026 — has reached 1,118 confirmed cases and 291 deaths, with no vaccine or specific treatment for this Ebola strain; the WHO simultaneously released its first comprehensive filovirus clinical management guidelines. Three seasons of real-world nirsevimab data from Yale-New Haven show 89% effectiveness against RSV hospitalization, waning to 40% at 12 months and tracking RSV's shifting seasonality. FDA approved tebipenem pivoxil (Utebzi) as the first oral carbapenem antibiotic in history, for complicated drug-resistant UTIs. AbbVie acquires Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9 billion for zumilokibart, a half-life-extended anti-IL-13 antibody in late-stage development for atopic dermatitis. The biomeStat agentic AI system ran a complete 1,000-genome dengue phylogenomic analysis in under 24 hours. Anthropic's Fable 5 model was suspended three days after launch via the first U.S. export control order ever applied to a commercial AI model. And a new Amazonian spider named Taczanowskia waska mimics the appearance and behavior of a spider-killing fungus — the first animal known to mimic a parasitic fungus.
item.itunes:summary: Bundibugyo Ebola PHEIC in DRC/Uganda — 1,118 cases, 291 deaths, no vaccine or specific treatment; WHO releases first comprehensive filovirus clinical management guidelines. Nirsevimab 3-season real-world data: 89% effective against RSV hospitalization, waning to 40% at 12 months with shifting RSV seasonality. FDA approves tebipenem pivoxil (Utebzi) — first oral carbapenem ever — for drug-resistant complicated UTIs. AbbVie acquires Apogee for $10.9B for zumilokibart, anti-IL-13 antibody for atopic dermatitis. biomeStat agentic AI: 1,000-genome dengue phylogenomics in 24 hours. Fable 5 suspended via first-ever U.S. export control order on a commercial AI model. Taczanowskia waska: Amazonian spider mimicking a spider-killing fungus.

item.title: Episode 3: Reading COVID's escape map
item.description: Deep mutational scanning of the JN.1 and XEC receptor-binding domains maps every possible antibody escape mutation — hotspots at positions 346, 456, and 493 converge with real-world evolution in KP.2 and KP.3, constrained by a fitness tradeoff at the ACE2-binding interface. A wastewater-based genomic surveillance workflow from Argentina uses information-theory diversity analysis to detect lineage-specific variants and biogeographical trends invisible to clinical sequencing. Ollin Biosciences raises $330 million Series B to advance OLN324 — a bispecific VEGF/angiopoietin-2 antibody that outperformed faricimab in the JADE head-to-head trial — into global Phase 3 for DME and wet AMD. And a whole-genome study of 43 leopards shows that the Cape Floristic Region's half-sized leopards are not inbred outcasts but a locally adapted population with 90 positively selected genes for body size and energy use, isolated for 20,000 years.
item.itunes:summary: Deep mutational scanning of JN.1/XEC RBD maps escape hotspots at positions 346, 456, 493 — already appearing in KP.2/KP.3, constrained by ACE2-binding fitness tradeoff. Wastewater genomics workflow from Argentina catches lineage-specific variants clinical sequencing misses. Ollin Biosciences $330M Series B for OLN324 bispecific VEGF/Ang2 antibody after head-to-head JADE trial win over faricimab. Cape Floristic Region leopards: 90 positively selected body-size genes, 20,000-year-old local adaptation, not inbreeding.

item.title: Episode 2: Why H5N1 went for the udder
item.description: Science Advances paper explains why H five N one attacked dairy cows' udders instead of their lungs — N-linked sialic acid receptors concentrated in mammary tissue and virtually absent in airways, giving the virus a predictive tissue-tropism framework. A new bioRxiv preprint identifies PfAP2-HCR, the master ApiAP2 transcription factor that switches on Plasmodium falciparum's host cell remodeling program; conditional loss arrests ring-stage development and collapses the PTEX export apparatus. MoonLake's sonelokimab nanobody delivers durable one-year Phase 3 data in hidradenitis suppurativa, with 67% achieving HiSCR75 and one-third complete resolution. Insilico Medicine and SK Biopharmaceuticals announce a $2.5 billion AI drug-discovery collaboration for neuroimmune CNS disorders. And a Waitomo cave fossil assemblage yields Strigops insulaborealis, a million-year-old relative of the kakapo that may have retained the ability to fly.
item.itunes:summary: Science Advances paper explains why H five N one attacked dairy cows' udders instead of their lungs — N-linked sialic acid receptors concentrated in mammary tissue, virtually absent in airways. PfAP2-HCR identified as the master transcription factor driving malaria parasite host cell remodeling; loss is lethal at ring stage. MoonLake sonelokimab: 67% HiSCR75 at one year in hidradenitis suppurativa. Insilico Medicine and SK Biopharmaceuticals $2.5 billion AI drug-discovery deal for neuroimmune CNS disorders. Strigops insulaborealis: a million-year-old flying ancestor of the kakapo from a Waitomo cave.

item.title: Episode 1: Hidden arboviruses in Kinshasa
item.description: Sero-survey out of Kinshasa Province finds 38% dengue and 24% chikungunya IgG seroprevalence with age-rising patterns, time-varying force of infection, and a peri-urban hotspot — strong evidence the DRC is sitting on substantial undetected arboviral circulation. Sanofi partners with Pangaea Data to mine US EHRs for undiagnosed alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, prepping the market for efdoralprin alfa. Sparse autoencoders applied to MolFormer reveal a clean layer-wise split: early layers parse SMILES grammar, later layers encode pharmacologically meaningful chemistry. And to close, genomics splits Japan's Ijima's leaf warbler into two species, naming the Tokara leaf warbler — Japan's first new bird in 40+ years, with one confirmed breeding island and immediate conservation implications.
item.itunes:summary: Sero-survey out of Kinshasa Province finds 38% dengue and 24% chikungunya IgG seroprevalence with age-rising patterns, time-varying force of infection, and a peri-urban hotspot. Sanofi partners with Pangaea Data to mine US EHRs for undiagnosed alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency. Sparse autoencoders applied to MolFormer reveal grammar-then-chemistry layer organization. Genomics splits Japan's Ijima's leaf warbler into two species — Japan's first new bird in 40+ years.
